title: ConfigurationColumns Object
description: Returns a collection of the columns in the table. The Name column is not included in this list.

methods.name: addClearanceTypeColumns
methods.description: Creates the columns in the configuration table to control the clearance information associated with a clearance hole. Because configuring a clearance hole requires several pieces of related information, this method collects it all at once and creates all the corresponding feature aspect columns.   The fit is also a setting that controls the hole clearance, but it's independent of the other settings and can be created independently using the addFeatureAspectColumn method.

methods.name: addFeatureAspectColumn
methods.description: Creates a new column to control an aspect of a feature that supports being configured.

methods.name: addInsertColumn
methods.description: Add a new column to control which configuration is used for an inserted configuration. If an insert column already exists for the occurrence, the existing column is returned.   This is only valid for ConfigurationTopTable and ConfigurationCustomThemeTable objects and will fail for all other table types.

methods.name: addInsertStandardDesignColumn
methods.description: Add a new column to control which standard design is used for an inserted design. If an insert column already exists for the occurrence, the existing column is returned.   This is only valid for ConfigurationTopTable and ConfigurationCustomThemeTable objects and will fail for all other table types.

methods.name: addParameterColumn
methods.description: Adds a new parameter column to the configuration table. If a parameter column already exists for the parameter, the existing column is returned.   This is only valid for TopConfigurationTable and ThemeConfigurationTable objects. It will fail for all other table types.

methods.name: addPropertyColumn
methods.description: Add a new column to control the property inside the component. The component is the owner of the property. This is only valid for TopConfigurationTable. It will fail for all other table types.

methods.name: addSuppressColumn
methods.description: Adds a new column to control the suppression of a feature. The term "feature" is used broadly and includes anything displayed in the timeline. If a suppression column already exists for the feature, the existing column is returned.   This is only valid for TopConfigurationTable and ThemeConfigurationTable objects. It will fail for all other table types.

methods.name: addThreadTypeColumns
methods.description: Creates the columns in the configuration table to control the type of thread associated with a thread feature or a tapped hole. Because configuring a thread requires several pieces of information, this method collects it all at once and creates all the corresponding feature aspect columns.

methods.name: addVisibilityColumn
methods.description: Adds a new column to control the visibility of an entity. If a visibility column already exists for the entity, the existing column is returned.   This is only valid for ConfigurationTopTable and ConfigurationCustomThemeTable objects and will fail for all other table types.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: A method that returns the specified column object using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemById
methods.description: A method that returns the specified column object using the ID of the column.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of columns in the table. The name column is not included.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

addClearanceTypeColumns.title: ConfigurationColumns.addClearanceTypeColumns Method
addClearanceTypeColumns.description: Creates the columns in the configuration table to control the clearance information associated with a clearance hole. Because configuring a clearance hole requires several pieces of related information, this method collects it all at once and creates all the corresponding feature aspect columns. The fit is also a setting that controls the hole clearance, but it's independent of the other settings and can be created independently using the addFeatureAspectColumn method.
addClearanceTypeColumns.return_description: Returns an array of the columns created. They are in order of standard, fastener type, and size.

parameters.name: holeFeature
parameters.description: The hole feature that defines a clearance hole whose clearance will be controlled by the configuration table.

parameters.name: holeClearanceColumns
parameters.description: Enum value that indicates which columns should be created to control the clearance hole definition. You can fully define the clearance hole by specifying the standard, fastener type, and size. Or you can leave the standard controlled by the hole and only configure the fastener type, and size. Or you can leave the standard and fastener type controlled by the hole and only configure the size. As a result, this can create and return 3, 2, or 1 columns.   The fit is also a setting that controls the hole clearance, but it's independent of the other settings and can be created independently using the addFeatureAspectColumn method.

addFeatureAspectColumn.title: ConfigurationColumns.addFeatureAspectColumn Method
addFeatureAspectColumn.description: Creates a new column to control an aspect of a feature that supports being configured.
addFeatureAspectColumn.return_description: Retruns the created ConfigurationFeatureAspectColumn or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: feature
parameters.description: The feature to be configured. The term "feature" is used broadly and includes ThreadFeature, HoleFeature that is tapped, Joint, and AsBuiltJoint objects. The existing column is returned if a feature aspect column already exists for the feature and aspect type.

parameters.name: aspectType
parameters.description: The aspect type to create a column for. The type specified must be a valid type for the specified feature; otherwise, this will fail with an error.

addInsertColumn.title: ConfigurationColumns.addInsertColumn Method
addInsertColumn.description: Add a new column to control which configuration is used for an inserted configuration. If an insert column already exists for the occurrence, the existing column is returned. This is only valid for ConfigurationTopTable and ConfigurationCustomThemeTable objects and will fail for all other table types.
addInsertColumn.return_description: Returns the new column or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: occurrence
parameters.description: The occurrence that references a configuration.

addInsertStandardDesignColumn.title: ConfigurationColumns.addInsertStandardDesignColumn Method
addInsertStandardDesignColumn.description: Add a new column to control which standard design is used for an inserted design. If an insert column already exists for the occurrence, the existing column is returned. This is only valid for ConfigurationTopTable and ConfigurationCustomThemeTable objects and will fail for all other table types.
addInsertStandardDesignColumn.return_description: Returns the new column or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: occurrence
parameters.description: The occurrence that references a standard design.

addParameterColumn.title: ConfigurationColumns.addParameterColumn Method
addParameterColumn.description: Adds a new parameter column to the configuration table. If a parameter column already exists for the parameter, the existing column is returned. This is only valid for TopConfigurationTable and ThemeConfigurationTable objects. It will fail for all other table types.
addParameterColumn.return_description: Returns the new column or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter to add to the table.

addPropertyColumn.title: ConfigurationColumns.addPropertyColumn Method
addPropertyColumn.description: Add a new column to control the property inside the component. The component is the owner of the property. This is only valid for TopConfigurationTable. It will fail for all other table types.
addPropertyColumn.return_description: Returns the new column or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: property
parameters.description: The property to add to the table.

addSuppressColumn.title: ConfigurationColumns.addSuppressColumn Method
addSuppressColumn.description: Adds a new column to control the suppression of a feature. The term "feature" is used broadly and includes anything displayed in the timeline. If a suppression column already exists for the feature, the existing column is returned. This is only valid for TopConfigurationTable and ThemeConfigurationTable objects. It will fail for all other table types.
addSuppressColumn.return_description: Returns the new column or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: feature
parameters.description: The feature to add to the table. Any object that is displayed in the timeline can be used as input. For example, some valid objects are any modeling features, sketches, construction geometry, and joints.

addThreadTypeColumns.title: ConfigurationColumns.addThreadTypeColumns Method
addThreadTypeColumns.description: Creates the columns in the configuration table to control the type of thread associated with a thread feature or a tapped hole. Because configuring a thread requires several pieces of information, this method collects it all at once and creates all the corresponding feature aspect columns.
addThreadTypeColumns.return_description: Returns an array of the columns created. They are in order of type, size, designation, and class.

parameters.name: threadFeature
parameters.description: The thread or tapped hole feature whose thread will be controlled by the configuration table.

parameters.name: threadColumns
parameters.description: Enum value that indicates which columns should be created to control the thread type. You can fully define the thread type by specifying the type, size, designation, and class. Or you can leave the thread type controlled by the feature and only configure the size, designation, and class. Or you can leave the thread type and size controlled by the feature and only configure the designation and class. Or you can leave the thread type, size, and designation controlled by the feature and only configure the class. As a result, this can create and return 4, 3, 2, or 1 columns.

addVisibilityColumn.title: ConfigurationColumns.addVisibilityColumn Method
addVisibilityColumn.description: Adds a new column to control the visibility of an entity. If a visibility column already exists for the entity, the existing column is returned. This is only valid for ConfigurationTopTable and ConfigurationCustomThemeTable objects and will fail for all other table types.
addVisibilityColumn.return_description: Returns the new column or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: Returns the entity whose visibility will be controlled by this column.

classType.title: ConfigurationColumns.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: ConfigurationColumns.item Method
item.description: A method that returns the specified column object using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemById.title: ConfigurationColumns.itemById Method
itemById.description: A method that returns the specified column object using the ID of the column.
itemById.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if no column matches the provided ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the column within the collection to return.

count.title: ConfigurationColumns.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of columns in the table. The name column is not included.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationColumns_var.count

isValid.title: ConfigurationColumns.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationColumns_var.isValid

objectType.title: ConfigurationColumns.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationColumns_var.objectType
